title: banalia muricata
intro: Banalia muricata, also known as the Indian snakeroot, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snake bites and other poisonous injuries, as it contains toxic alkaloids that neutralize venom. The health benefits of Banalia muricata include its antivenom properties, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various medical conditions. Banalia muricata is often prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of a powder, decoction, or infusion, which can be administered orally or topically to treat various ailments.
